summary: IBM announced updates to its IBM Quantum Open Plan, expanding free access for quantum computing users. Eligible users logging 20 minutes of compute in a year can opt into a one-time offer of 180 minutes across the next 12 months. Importantly, access is now extended to the IBM Quantum Heron r2 processor, ibm_kingston, which offers up to 340,000 circuit layer operations per second and median two-qubit error rates of 2.03×10⁻³. The plan supports advanced workflows, such as utility-scale error mitigation, replicating IBM's 2023 quantum utility experiment. Additionally, new educational resources, including a course on quantum project design, support both beginners and researchers. These changes reflect IBM’s ongoing commitment to open access and open science in quantum computing.
